title: mutisia subulata
intro: Mutisia subulata, also known as the "Rock rose" or "Tree daisy", is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Asteraceae family. It is native to the Andean region of South America and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues. The plant contains flavonoids and terpenes, which have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, providing several health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and boosting the immune system. Mutisia subulata can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms and promote overall well-being.
